Question: Figure 1.4 says the mass of the atmosphere is 10^19 kg. Assuming the density of the atmosphere is 1 kg/m^3, estimate the height of Earth’s atmosphere. Do you think your answer is an underestimate or an overestimate? Explain why.
Answer: $$3 \times 10^{4} \text{m}$$ or 30 km. It is probably an underestimate because the density of the atmosphere decreases with altitude. (In fact, 30 km does not even get us out of the stratosphere.)
